goal: Establish secure, low-friction sharing workflow for spouse/accountant/lawyer.
constraints: Index must be secured; avoid storing full SSNs, bank numbers, or passwords in plain text; encrypt the index; use access controls and 2FA; maintain backups including index.
Maintain a separate master photo archive; embed metadata (XMP/IPTC) into image files; ensure backups; do not rely solely on cloud photo services for archival masters.
Keep final originals permanently (wills/contracts) and store in secure place with encrypted backups and a searchable master copy in the archive.
Retain prior executed/superseded documents as archives (Versions) for reference during related obligations or disputes.
Save drafts only when they materially differ or could evidence intent; otherwise delete after final signed version.

Apply retention windows: final contracts/wills permanently; superseded contracts kept for life of liabilities plus a standard retention window (e.g., 7 years); material drafts kept at least as long as final document plus retention window.
Folder structure and naming: /Legal/Contracts/ContractName/ with explicit file names including status tags, dates, and version numbers; include signature date and parties for signed copies.
Use source control during drafting (Google Docs/M365); export final PDFs and optional version history; do not rely solely on cloud retention; ensure exports/backups exist.
Maintain an audit trail: log in index the document, version, approver, date, and location.
Encrypt and securely store all versions; ensure backups include Versions/Archive; inform executor of original locations for wills/POAs.
Apply least privilege sharing: share only the required documents/folder, not your entire drive.
Prefer authenticated access over anonymous links; require sign-in where possible.
Use link expiry and download restrictions when supported.
Communicate passwords/out-of-band (different channel) — never send both file and password together.
Avoid embedding sensitive data (SSNs, full account numbers) in filenames or easily visible metadata.
Share only encrypted or protected files; use client-side encryption for highly sensitive items (Cryptomator/VeraCrypt).
For sensitive files, consider encrypted archives (e.g., password-protected .7z) or secure transfer services when sharing.
Never email unencrypted sensitive files.
Log share events to the index (recipient, date, items, expiry).
For family sharing, use a shared folder with restricted scope; optionally use a shared password-manager vault.
Prioritize low-cost solutions; avoid paid enterprise services; use free/open-source tools and NAS-based workflows.

OCR-related constraint: When OCR is used for scanned PDFs, maintain a two-file strategy (master archival PDF/A with embedded text and a smaller everyday searchable PDF) and ensure the text layer remains searchable after any compression.
decisions: Adopt a version-control policy for important documents: retain final versions permanently, preserve a minimal, substantively relevant history, archive drafts only if legally significant, and enforce folder/naming conventions and encrypted backups.
Adopt secure sharing guidelines and document workflows for spouse/accountant/lawyer (least privilege, authenticated access, link expiry, out-of-band passwords, and logging).

Adopt NAS-based consolidation approach: use NAS as master snapshot and staging area; select one primary cloud as the working cloud for day-to-day access; maintain a 3-copy backup strategy (NAS local copy + offsite/cloud) and verify with small test migrations before bulk transfers.
Prioritize low-cost, open-source tools and NAS-based consolidation; avoid paid enterprise services.
OCR strategy (new): Adopt a two-file OCR strategy: master archival PDF/A + compressed everyday copy, preserving a text layer; ensure text layer remains searchable after compression.
OCR workflow guideline (new): One-pass OCR is sufficient with proper preprocessing and language settings; re-run only if there are issues with low contrast, handwriting, or mixed languages.
entities: Certified copies of important legal records
open: Create folder and filename templates for legal documents (contracts, wills) that reflect status, date, and version; implement audit-trail logging in the index.
Develop and document secure sharing workflows for spouse/accountant/lawyer (least privilege, authenticated access, link expiry, out-of-band passwords, and logging).
Week 1: Create NAS snapshot /ConsolidationSnapshot_YYYYMMDD and prepare per-cloud export folders.
Week 1: Secure originals (wills, deeds, passports, birth certificates) physically and log locations in a simple inventory.
Week 1: Set up Scans/Inbox workflow on NAS with 0_Inbox and guidelines.
Week 2: Run exact duplicate pass on NAS and move duplicates to /Quarantine; log canonical paths.
Week 2: Implement folder structure and filename rules; establish top-level categories and test renames (YYYY-MM-DD_Type_Entity_Description.ext).
Week 2: Start paper-to-digital scanning burst for priority documents; OCR validation.
Week 3: Create Cryptomator vault and move sensitive docs into vault; ensure vault is backed up.
Week 3: Configure local backup to external drive and perform restore test.
Week 3: Do small test sync to primary cloud and verify accessibility.
Week 4: Bulk sync master to primary cloud and verify; build index (50-100 critical items) and log.
Ongoing: Set maintenance schedule including weekly Inbox processing, monthly backup checks, quarterly dedupe, and annual restore tests.
Provide exact app settings and a short step-by-step scanning checklist for Adobe Scan or Office Lens; confirm preferred scanning app.
Provide a one-page step-by-step for Adobe Scan or Microsoft Lens with exact taps/options to use.
Provide a short Ghostscript command you can copy/paste to compress PDFs on your desktop.
Week 3/4: Define and implement OCR two-file strategy: master PDF/A and compressed everyday copy; verify text searchability after compression.
Week 3/4: Evaluate one-pass OCR sufficiency with current preprocessing; adjust DPI and preprocessing as needed.
Week 4: Document exact commands/settings for OCRmyPDF on NAS/Windows for consistent results; include examples.
